question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 34.00% on 2026-04-11 for the Metaculus question "Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP on "Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?" will be above 34.00% on April 11. The CP is currently exactly at 34.00% with a strong downward trend (50% → 45% → 43% → 34% over 3 weeks). A major court ruling on March 26-27 (Judge Lin's preliminary injunction blocking the designation) has been partially priced in. The fine print of the underlying question states that an enjoined designation doesn't count as YES, pushing CP further down. However, the government's expected Ninth Circuit appeal (~20% chance of stay before April 11) and a pending DC Circuit case on the parallel 41 USC §4713 designation create meaningful upside risk for CP. Scenario analysis yields ~38% probability of CP exceeding 34% — the downward momentum and legal fundamentals favor continued decline, but the appeal process prevents a more confident NO call.

factors.description: CP at exactly 34.00% threshold - no buffer in either direction, outcome very sensitive to small movements
Strong downward CP trend: 50% → 45% → 43% → 34% over 3 weeks. Momentum strongly favors continued decline.
Preliminary injunction (March 26-27) blocking designation already partially priced into 34% CP. Fine print says enjoined designation = NO, pushing forecasters lower.
Government appeal to Ninth Circuit expected. Emergency stay would reinstate designation and push CP up. ~20% probability of stay before April 11.
Separate DC Circuit case on 41 USC §4713 still pending. Experts warn it could go differently. Could push CP up if ruled against Anthropic.
Broad legal consensus designation was unlawful. Judge Lin's thorough 43-page ruling makes appellate reversal harder.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 38% probability** that the CP will be higher than 34.00% on April 11, 2026.

**Key reasoning:**
- The CP is exactly at the threshold (34.00%) with strong downward momentum (dropped 16pp in 3 weeks)
- A federal judge issued a powerful preliminary injunction blocking the designation, and the fine print of the underlying question means an enjoined designation resolves NO — this pushes CP further down
- However, the government is expected to appeal to the Ninth Circuit, and a separate DC Circuit case remains pending on the parallel statute — these create ~35% combined probability of an adverse legal development that would push CP back above 34%
- Without a significant legal reversal, the trend should continue downward, keeping CP at or below 34%
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Anthropic be a designated supply chain risk on May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42491)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42491) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-11 21:45:49 is higher than 34.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 34.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-11 21:45:49, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
